payload.claim: Hypertext Markup Language (HTML) is the standard markup language for documents designed to be displayed in a web browser. It defines the content and structure of web content.

HTML provides a means to create structured documents by denoting structural semantics for text such as headings, paragraphs, lists, links, quotes. Elements are delineated by tags written using angle brackets.
HTML was specified by Tim Berners-Lee in late 1990 based on an earlier 1989 memo proposing an Internet-based hypertext system. The initial design was strongly influenced by CERN SGML.
